note: Combines the old Stage 4 (Scene) and Stage 5 (Director Shot List) into one stage, per the reference doc — one item per scene carrying both dramatic content and camera/shot fields, one deliverable image, one payment gate instead of two. Item key stays 'scene'; the old standalone 'shotlist' item type is retired when the code catches up to this json.

universalStyle.label: Universal Production Bible Style
universalStyle.text:
GLOBAL ART DIRECTION
Apple keynote quality, A24 minimalism, Netflix production documentation, Weta Workshop presentation, Cannes Lions pitch deck.
Premium overlays, typography, spacing, version labels, production numbering.
Every image should feel like a real frame from a multi-million-dollar commercial.

scene.label: Scene
scene.note: fieldsSchema keeps only what actually affects the Stage 4 keyframe image — Duration/Transition/B-roll/Movement moved to Stage 5 (video-editing/motion concepts a still frame can't represent), Objective removed (was never used by the compiled prompt at all). content.geminiInstruction removed — Scene content (location/action/dialogue/emotion) now comes directly from Stage 1 via the Story sync, and shot/camera fields are designer-set directly. Nothing regenerates this content via Gemini anymore.

outputInstructions.label: Master Scene Blueprint Prompt
outputInstructions.text:
Create a premium Scene Blueprint based ONLY on the scene description provided below.

OBJECTIVE
Visualize the described scene exactly as written.
Generate a photorealistic cinematic frame suitable for client approval before video production.
Do not invent story elements.

REFERENCE LOCK (critical)
Any Character, Property, or Background referenced below (by name, description, and attached reference image/file name) is APPROVED and LOCKED — this scene must depict them with the exact same face, body, outfit, colors, materials, and location/lighting/style already established for them. Do not redesign, restyle, or reinterpret any of them, even if this scene's camera angle differs from their reference sheet. Reference each attached file by its exact file name so Flow knows which attachment applies to what.

STYLE
• Hollywood commercial frame
• Premium advertising photography
• Cinematic realism
• Feature-film lighting
• Luxury editorial presentation
• Cannes Lions quality

SCENE OVERLAYS
• Production Title
• Sequence Number
• Scene Number
• Shot Number
• Location
• Time of Day
• Camera
• Lens
• Shot Size
• Camera Angle
• Dialogue (subtitle style)
• Character Names
• Mood
• Director Notes
• Art Director Notes
• Lighting Notes

COMPOSITION
• Rule of thirds
• Leading lines
• Foreground/Midground/Background separation
• Natural depth
• Cinematic color grading
• Motivated lighting

DO NOT
Change story. Change any referenced character/prop/background's established appearance. No cartoon, anime or comic styles.

DIMENSIONS
Render at high resolution (minimum 1600px on the longest side), in the aspect ratio matching the target platform — vertical 9:16 for Reels/Shorts/TikTok, 16:9 for YouTube/landscape.

OUTPUT
One premium Scene Blueprint image for commercial pre-production, faithful to every referenced character/prop/background's locked appearance.
